q:
Consider the following reaction sequence

The product 'B' is
question:
Consider the following reaction sequence

​​​​​​​

The product 'B' is
solution:
Diisobutylaluminium hydride, AlH(i-Bu)2  also known as DIBAL or DIBAL-H, is an exceedingly useful and versatile reducing agent. It is an electrophilic reducing agent, usually employed in selective reductions of esters or nitriles to aldehydes

So, in first step nitrile group will be reduced to aldehyde group. 

In second step cross aldol condensation reaction will take place which will give mixture of products. Out of which one of the product will be option B.

subject: Chemistry
q:
D-(+)-Glyceraldehyde→ii)H2O/H+iii)HNO3i)HCN

The products formed in the above reaction are
question:
D-(+)-Glyceraldehyde→ii)H2O/H+iii)HNO3i)HCN

The products formed in the above reaction are
options: One optically active and one meso product
One optically inactive and one meso product
solution: The aldehyde group of glyceraldehyde reacts with hydrogen cyanide give cyanohydrin. Two isomers formed in the above step, because aldehyde is planar and changed to tetrahedral centre. On hydrolysis cyanide group convert to carboxylic acid. Upon reaction with nitric acid, the primary alcoholic group undergo oxidation to carboxylic acid group. One compound is meso compound, optically inactive and other compound is optically active.

q: m-chlorobenzaldehyde on treatment with 50%KOH solution yields
question: m-chlorobenzaldehyde on treatment with 50%KOH solution yields
solution:
Meta–chlorobenzaldehyde will undergo Cannizzaro reaction with 50% KOH to give mchlorobenzoate ion and m-chlorobenzyl alcohol. 

Since m-chlorobenzaldehyde does not contain α hydrogen thus with 50% KOH it undergoes the Cannizzaro reaction. The Cannizzaro reaction for the m-chlorobenzaldehyde is given as:

Since it is a disproportionation reaction so one molecule of m-chlorobenzaldehyde is reduced to m-chlorobenzyl alcohol at the cost of others which is oxidized potassium m-chlorobenzoate.

mw of benzaldehyde $=106$ $106 \times 3=318 \mathrm{gm}$. Benzaldehyde is required to give 1.5 mole (or $351 \mathrm{gm}$ ) product

question: Which one of the following reactions does not represent correct combination of substrate and product under the given conditions?
solution: Cyanide on reduction with DIBAL-H gives aldehyde. Acid halide with rosenmund reduction reaction gives aldehyde. So, all these reaction are correct except benzyl alcohol with oxidation with strong oxidising agents sodium dichromate will give carboxylic acid not the aldehyde.

question: Tollen's reagent and Fehling solutions are used to distinguish between

All aldehydes and ketones show reaction with Tollen's reagent and Fehling solutions, but ketones do not show this reaction.

q: Which reactant will give the following alcohol on reaction with one mole of phenyl magnesium bromide PhMgBr followed by acidic hydrolysis?
question: Which reactant will give the following alcohol on reaction with one mole of phenyl magnesium bromide PhMgBr followed by acidic hydrolysis?
solution: Phenyl magnesium bromide is called Grignard reagent. One mole of this react with carbonyl compound to give alcohols. It react with aldehyde to form secondary alcohol while with ketones gives tertiary alcohols. The given compound is a tertiary alcohol so, reactant should be a ketone.

q: Identify the name reaction.
question: Identify the name reaction.
solution:
Gattermann – Koch reaction is used for the industrial production of benzaldehyde. 

In Gattermann – Koch reaction, benzene is treated with carbon monoxide in the acidic medium in presence of anhydrous aluminium chloride to give benzaldehyde. In this reaction, anhydrous aluminium chloride works as a catalyst. It is an electrophilic substitution reaction.
